card.title: mazer: cross-viewport UI reliability and layout hardening
card.summary: Coordinate mobile safe-area, overlay containment, browser layout, and touch-control behavior through one viewport contract and physical-device proof path.
card.objective: Keep every Mazer surface contained and usable across desktop, narrow browser, phone browser, and installed PWA while preserving one deterministic control-intent contract.
acceptanceCriteria: Every primary Mazer surface has an owner, supported viewport category, and no-overlap contract.
Mobile and browser layout work can be verified independently and together through a shared matrix.
No completion claim relies on desktop-only proof for a phone or restored-window defect.
Player Guide content remains contained and held stick/arrow intent remains recoverable on the physical-phone surface.
discoveries: The compact guide card began six pixels behind its viewport mask after scrollbar-center adjustment.
Blocked held movement canceled its repeat timer, and MenuScene discarded the stick's ordered dominant/fallback candidates through a coarser analog recomputation.
A weak analog secondary must be a wall-only fallback rather than an eager queued turn at a legal T-junction branch.
nextActions: Review and merge Mazer PR #77 without mixing unrelated product work.
After a separately authorized production release, verify guide containment, one-tile wall fallback, T-junction behavior, and sliding arrows on the operator's physical iPhone.
Keep installed-PWA banding and native browser-chrome/maximize proof open.
blockers: Physical iPhone post-release feel and containment verification is pending.
evidence: Focused TypeScript/control/UI/architecture proof passed 5 files / 84 tests.
Clean full Mazer verification passed 49 files / 367 tests plus Vite/PWA build.
Isolated 390x844 browser proof passed guide alignment, real T-junction dominant-lane continuity, beyond-frame and center arrow capture, blocked retry, release cleanup, and zero page/console errors.

entry.headline: Mobile guide and held-control rework is review-ready
completed: Aligned the compact Player Guide with its viewport mask.
Separated decisive queued turns from weak analog wall-only fallbacks.
Kept captured arrow and stick repeats alive while blocked and preserved arrow capture beyond the frame and through the center.
Completed focused, full-repository, build, and isolated browser proof.
discovered: Earlier deterministic proof did not supersede the operator's later physical-device regression evidence.
The active coordinating card must remain below completion until the repaired production build is checked on the physical iPhone.
next: Run physical-iPhone post-release verification before lifecycle completion or marker ratchet.
blockers: Physical iPhone post-release verification remains pending.
